arguments.argument: The core value of Privacy cannot be reconciled with the pursuit of "true" preferences, as it fundamentally undermines the autonomy and agency of individuals. While the allure of gaining insights into human behavior is tempting, the act of simulating users based on their digital footprints is a profound invasion of privacy. Even with safeguards, the system risks normalizing the exploitation of personal data, creating a feedback loop where users are subtly coerced into aligning with algorithmic interpretations of their "true" selves. This erodes the very foundation of consent, as users may not fully grasp the long-term implications of their data being weaponized to shape their choices. Privacy is not a hindrance to wisdom—it is the ethical bedrock that ensures wisdom is exercised responsibly.
